title: Siargao Boat Rental Costs: A 2026 Guide to Prices & Charters
html:
With its world-class surf breaks and turquoise lagoons, Siargao is best explored from the water. Renting a boat from General Luna costs from EUR 120 for a half-day island hopping trip, and BoatHire24 makes it simple to compare options. Whether you want a shaded outrigger or a sleek motor yacht, knowing the local rates ensures a smooth booking.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Siargao?

A half-day island-hopping tour in a traditional bangka costs EUR 120 to EUR 180 for up to six passengers. For a private motorboat with a skipper, expect EUR 250 to EUR 400 for a full day. High-end options like the Princess V58 start from EUR 1,200 per day. You can book a charter online to compare verified boat listings and secure your preferred vessel.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Siargao?

Most rental boats in Siargao come with a skipper, so no licence is required for the charterer. If you wish to operate a small boat independently, you need an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or a local permit. The rental operator confirms exact requirements at the time of booking. Always confirm the licensing policy when you review the listing.

Where to set off in Siargao

The main departure point is General Luna, with its many dive shops and tour operators. Nearby Malinao Port serves as a hub for larger boats heading to Bucas Grande Island. For a quieter start, the bay at Dapa offers access to the northern islands. Many charters also leave from the shallows off Cloud 9 surf spot. Real entities include Siargao Island, the Pacific Ocean, and the town of Dapa.

Which boat to rent

  1. Bangka (outrigger canoe), best for day trips to Naked Island and Daku Island; seats up to 8 passengers; from EUR 150 per day.
  2. Small motorboat, ideal for fishing or surfing safari; carries 4 to 6 people; from EUR 250 per half day.
  3. Catamaran, spacious and stable for large groups; accommodates up to 12 guests; from EUR 600 for a full-day charter.
  4. Crewed yacht, offers luxury for longer expeditions around the Pacific; sleeps 6 to 8; from EUR 1,500 per day. For an example of a high-end option, check the Princess V58 for hire on BoatHire24.

Siargao boat rental costs in detail

  • Shared island-hopping tour (bangka): EUR 20 to EUR 30 per person for a full day.
  • Private small motorboat (half-day, up to 6 pax): EUR 250 to EUR 350.
  • Catamaran full-day charter (up to 12 pax): EUR 600 to EUR 900.
  • Crewed luxury yacht (full day, 6-8 pax): EUR 1,200 to EUR 1,800.

Best season to rent a boat in Siargao

The prime season runs from March to October, with the calmest seas between April and August. During these months the Amihan wind (northeast monsoon) gives way to the Habagat (southwest monsoon), creating excellent conditions. Typhoons are rare in summer. To find your ideal vessel, use the boat search on BoatHire24 and filter by date and boat type.

For the best snorkelling, head to Sugba Lagoon in the morning when the water is clearest and the crowds are thin.

At a glance

  • Price range: EUR 20 per person to EUR 1,800 per day
  • Peak months: March to October
  • Common trip length: half day (4 hours) or full day (8 hours)
  • Max capacity: up to 12 passengers on a catamaran
  • Currency: EUR (local prices quoted in PHP but we use EUR for consistency)

Questions about boat rental in Siargao

Can I rent a boat without a skipper?

Only a small number of operators allow self-drive rentals, and they require an ICC or equivalent licence, so skippered charters are the norm.

What should I bring for a full-day charter?

Pack sunscreen, a hat, swimwear, a dry bag for electronics, and enough drinking water for the day.

How far is it from General Luna to Sugba Lagoon?

Sugba Lagoon is about 18 nautical miles from General Luna, a trip of roughly 45 minutes by speedboat.

Is it possible to rent a boat for a multi-day trip?

Yes, several operators offer multi-day charters to places like Bucas Grande Island and the Sohoton Cove area.

Whether you are planning a family day out or a luxury cruise, Siargao’s waters await. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Siargao or list your own. To begin, check availability and explore listings from trusted hosts. If you own a boat, you can become a host and earn by listing it on the platform.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Siargao and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
